title: RX callback snapshot prevents early mutex release
description:
lora_l2.c:148-151 locks lora_mutex just to snapshot the callback pointer, then immediately unlocks. This is correct but inefficient: the mutex is held only for the snapshot, yet the callback is invoked outside the mutex anyway.

What goes wrong as-is: Nothing breaks, but on every RX packet the thread acquires/releases a mutex purely to copy two pointers. At SF10 with 5s timeout, this is not a hot path, but the pattern obscures intent.

Alternative: Use atomic pointer exchange or a single atomic containing both pointers (RCU-style) to avoid the mutex on the read path. Or accept the mutex as documenting the invariant that cb+user_data are always updated together.
close_reason: Opinion: current mutex pattern is correct, micro-optimization not worth complexity
